title: Removing Signal connection is impossible
author: fm-sys
body: ### Guidelines - [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates - [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended - [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic ### Bug description Removing a Signal connection is impossible in certain conditions. I am seeing Stories of a person I don't know, and it seems there is no way to remove that person. AFAIK I should only see stories of persons were I accepted the message request. However I've never accepted a message request for that person. Either there were ways how a contact could get accepted automatically under certain circumstances, or the person was in my contact list years ago. I would just like to delete the signal connection, however I couldn't find a way to do so: - It doesn't appear in my chat list, as we've never sent a message to each other. - It doesn't show up in global search. - Searching the number via "new chat" brings me directly to the conversation page without a delete option. - The conversation page doesn't offer a way to delete the conversation in the drop-down menu. - The conversation settings don't offer a delete option, only "block" and "report". I would like to just find a regular "delete" option for that signal connection, however, it seems like I'm stuck in that weird in-between state were it exists and not exists at the same time and I can't imagine that's intentional. (I'm using the beta versions BTW, but report it here as it's unrelated to the newest beta) ### Screenshots ### Device Google Pixel 7a ### Android version 17 ### Signal version 8.19.1 ### Link to debug log https://debuglogs.org/android/8.19.1/4b3fbde0b18d9f301d2d9a780eeee66bb72ec9a6f5c8d37f2ed655e64bcc86fc

title: CJKV names cut short in group chat message preview
author: larouxn
body: ### Guidelines - [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates - [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended - [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic ### Bug description Names such as 三上 (family, _mikami_) 純子 (given, _junko_), due to some formatting error within the Signal Desktop and Signal Android and presumably iOS as well applications, have their shortened in this case to just 子 in group chat message previews in the chat list. This is incorrect. Reported in Signal-Desktop as well: https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-Desktop/issues/7949 ### Screenshots ### Device Google Pixel 7 ### Android version 17 ### Signal version 8.18.2 ### Link to debug log _No response_

title: Phone number screen does not switch to RTL layout with ar-XB
author: ByteBreeze233
body: ### Guidelines - [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates - [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended - [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic ### Bug description After switching the system locale to `ar-XB`, the phone number screen remains in a left-to-right layout instead of updating to RTL. ### Screenshots ### Device Pixel 9 ### Android version 15 ### Signal version 8.15.0 ### Link to debug log _No response_

title: Cannot create v2 backups: NullPointerException in requireNonNullBlob()
author: tbvdm
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

On an old phone, after upgrading to v2 backups, Signal is no longer able to create backups. It used to be able to create "v1" backups (.backup files) without problems.

This is the relevant part of the debug log (I'm not at liberty to share the full debug log, but more information can be provided on request):

```
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob: java.lang.NullPointerException: Attempt to invoke virtual method 'java.lang.Class java.lang.Object.getClass()' on a null object reference
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.signal.core.util.CursorExtensionsKt.requireNonNullBlob(CursorExtensions.kt:62)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.database.AttachmentTable.getLocalArchivableAttachments(AttachmentTable.kt:628)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository.$r8$lambda$-JkYeh5kBb-_Hj7tMEfN6Eqopns(BackupRepository.kt:789)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository$$ExternalSyntheticLambda36.invoke(R8$$SyntheticClass:0)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository.export(BackupRepository.kt:1101)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository.exportForLocalBackup(BackupRepository.kt:777)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.local.LocalArchiver.export(LocalArchiver.kt:84)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.jobs.LocalArchiveJob.run(LocalArchiveJob.kt:102)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.jobmanager.JobRunner.run(JobRunner.java:99)
[8.17.4] [4242 ] 2026-07-06 16:13:46.593 GMT+02:00 W LocalArchiveJob:   at org.thoughtcrime.securesms.jobmanager.JobRunner.run(JobRunner.java:62)
```

This is the relevant part of AttachmentTable.kt:

```
    617   fun getLocalArchivableAttachments(): List<LocalArchivableAttachment> {
    618     return readableDatabase
    619       .select(*PROJECTION_WITH_METADATA)
    620       .from(TABLE_NAME_WITH_METADTA)
    621       .where("$DATA_HASH_END IS NOT NULL AND $DATA_FILE IS NOT NULL AND ${AttachmentMetadataTable.TABLE_NAME}.${AttachmentMetadataTable.LOCAL_BACKUP_KEY} IS NOT NULL")
    622       .orderBy("$TABLE_NAME.$ID DESC")
    623       .run()
    624       .readToList {
    625         LocalArchivableAttachment(
    626           attachmentId = AttachmentId(it.requireLong(ID)),
    627           file = File(it.requireNonNullString(DATA_FILE)),
    628           random = it.requireNonNullBlob(DATA_RANDOM),
    629           size = it.requireLong(DATA_SIZE),
    630           localBackupKey = AttachmentMetadataTable.getMetadata(it)!!.localBackupKey!!,
    631           plaintextHash = Base64.decode(it.requireNonNullString(DATA_HASH_END)),
    632           contentType = it.requireString(CONTENT_TYPE)
    633         )
    634       }
    635   }
```

The phone contains old attachments that are encrypted with ClassicDecryptingPartInputStream. For these attachments, the DATA_RANDOM column is NULL. I expect this is the problem.

Would it be possible to fix this one way or the other so that Signal on this phone can create backups again?

Thanks.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

_No response_

### Android version

_No response_

### Signal version

_No response_

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: Crash loop due to donations cancellation
author: matt3o
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Due to switching to the backup subscription I cancelled my donations on Paypal. These days the app registered that and since then I'm in a permanent crash loop and the app is unusable.
I can't even submit the debug log sadly, Signal crashes as soon as it is connected to the internet. I'll upload the relevant part here, if you need any more I can upload the full log file (was unsure if it contains sensitive information).

Any help would be great, this makes the app currently unusable!

title: MTN Nigeria - CAPTCHA passes but SMS/voice verification code never arrives (iOS)
author: Zenskistudios
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

## Summary

I'm unable to register Signal on an iPhone using an MTN Nigeria phone number.

The CAPTCHA completes successfully, but the SMS verification code is never delivered. I also tried the **"Call me instead"** option, but no verification call is received.

This appears to match the issue already reported for Android (Signal-Android issue #13573), suggesting this may be a broader MTN Nigeria verification delivery problem rather than an iPhone-specific issue.

## Environment

* **Country:** Nigeria
* **Carrier:** MTN Nigeria
* **Device:** [e.g. iPhone 13]
* **iOS version:** [e.g. iOS 17.4]
* **Signal version:** [Settings → About]
* **Debug log:** [Paste your https://debuglogs.org/... link]

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install Signal on an iPhone.
2. Start registration using an MTN Nigeria number (+234...).
3. Complete the CAPTCHA successfully.
4. Wait for the SMS verification code (it never arrives).
5. Select **"Call me instead."**
6. Wait for the verification call (it never arrives).

## Expected Result

A verification SMS or voice call should be delivered so registration can be completed.

## Actual Result

Neither the verification SMS nor the voice call is received, making it impossible to register with an MTN Nigeria number.

## Additional Information

Since a similar issue has already been reported on Android (issue #13573), this may indicate a carrier-side or verification delivery problem affecting MTN Nigeria across multiple platforms.


### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

_No response_

### Android version

_No response_

### Signal version

_No response_

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: Nigeria (MTN) — CAPTCHA passes but no SMS code received; voice call verification also fails
author: Zenskistudios
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

I'm having the same problem with MTN Nigeria.

CAPTCHA completes successfully, but the SMS verification code never arrives. 
I also tried "Call me instead" (voice verification) as a workaround — same 
result, no call comes through either.

Environment:
- Country: Nigeria
- Carrier: MTN
- Device: [YOUR PHONE MODEL, e.g. Tecno Spark 10]
- Android version: [e.g. Android 13]
- Signal app version: [Settings > Help > About Signal]
- Debug log: [PASTE YOUR debuglogs.org LINK HERE]

Steps to reproduce:
1. Install Signal on Android
2. Start registration with an MTN Nigeria number (+234...)
3. Solve CAPTCHA
4. Wait for SMS — never arrives
5. Tap "Call me instead" — call never comes through either

This seems to affect MTN Nigeria users broadly, not just me.

title: Backup timestamp in UTC
author: uok
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Hello,

I switched to the new backup format (folder based) and it works really fast (and saves space)!
The only thing I noticed is that the folder name timestamp is in UTC which is confusing.

example:
local time "2026-07-07 07:38:26"
backup folder "signal-backup-2026-07-07-05-38-26"

title: Gallery exits folder after app context switch
author: FormularSumo
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Steps to reproduce:

1. In a chat, press the plus button
2. Click on gallery
3. Open on a folder, such as "all files"
4. Switch to a different app or to the homescreen, and then back to Signal

Expected results:

- The selected folder is kept open

Actual results:

- The selected folder is closed and Signal reverts back to the main gallery screen. This is true even if I have selected photo(s) to attach (these selections are saved).

Additional context:
I usually come across this issue when I'm sending multiple photos to someone, such as when I'm selecting a few of the best ones after a trip or an event. I frequently switch between Signal and my photos app in order to see the photos properly (rather than just the grid preview), zoom in on them, etc. Currently, Signal closes the folder I have open (camera, all files) every time I go back to my photos app, which is quite annoying and makes the process take much longer than it should.

Comparison with other apps:
I've tested this on a few different messaging apps, and Signal is unique in closing the currently open folder. This behaviour doesn't happen on WhatsApp, Messenger, Google Messages, or Discord (uses the system file picked). It also doesn't happen on the iOS Signal app, which keeps the current folder (under Collections) and other options open.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Pixel 7

### Android version

Android 17

### Signal version

8.16.1

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: Continuous chat icon on home screen not going away
author: Techrese
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

oneplus nord 2ce
oxygenos 13.0
signal android version: 8.15.3

I am using an oneplus nord 2CE and recently i cam across something i can't seem to figure out.
You can add chats as a shortcut to your home screen, but I can't seem to remove them.

**What I did:**
goto chat -> Add to home screen
remove the icon by swiping it  to the trash (or in my case to the cross in the bottom middle)
Get a notification in that specific chat and the shortcut icon popups again.

**Expected:**
shortcut removed when Removed from home screen

I don't know if it is intended or not but I like to get it permanently removed from my home screen even when i get a notification, even when the icon is there i can still click on 'add to home screen'


### Device

oneplus nord2 ce

### Android version

13.0

### Signal version

8.15.3

title: Very high amounts of data usage
author: mudfreak-bz
body: ### Guidelines - [x] I have searched open and closed issues for duplicates - [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended - [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic ### Bug description I have a problem with very high amounts of data transfers with Signal. See this screenshot ( it is every month more or less like it). I have a number of active chats every day, but are almost completely only text messages (no video, no voice, very few media file exchanges). This is a problem, because if I'm not connected to WiFi Signal uses up my mobile data plan in no time. I have 2 connected devices (one uses Molly on Android, the other is Signal Desktop). I have read through https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-iOS/issues/3962 but I don't see anything there that solves the issue. I'm very happy to get any hints about what to try to narrow down the problem. ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Device Fairphone 3 ### Android version 15 // eOS 4.0-a15-20260610633983-official-FP3 ### Signal version 8.8.2 ### Link to debug log https://debuglogs.org/android/8.8.2/a82fdc33479571744bb8a081c4d593b662f2ae8ff87af5117d0cc6ed2389bdbb
